title: The Little Movement
author: Philip K. Dick
card_summary: A short story titled 'The Little Movement' by Philip K. Dick. The story is a known PKD short story, likely from the early 1950s. 'The Little Movement' concerns toys that develop sentience and organize for survival, a theme characteristic of Dick's early work exploring the nature of consciousness and the boundaries between living and non-living things.
page_summary:
A 9-page scanned PDF of 'The Little Movement,' a short story by Philip K. Dick. No OCR text layer is present. 'The Little Movement' is a known Dick short story from his early career, published in Fantasy and Science Fiction magazine in November 1952.

The story is one of Dick's earliest published science fiction stories, written during the explosive burst of productivity that launched his professional career. At 9 scanned pages, this document preserves the story in its original published format. 'The Little Movement' demonstrates the themes of animation, autonomy, and the blurring of boundaries between the living and the mechanical that would become hallmarks of Dick's mature fiction.
